brand.taglineShort: Build your home gym right the first time.

methodology: Every item purchased at retail and tested for 60+ days of regular training use — no floor models or manufacturer loaners.
Load testing: barbells tested to rated capacity, racks tested for stability under working load, cardio machines run for 30+ hours.
We photograph assembly and note actual time and difficulty — not manufacturer estimates.
Price-per-quality cliff is identified for each category: above this price, you're paying for branding not performance.
Reader failure reports are taken seriously: if three readers report the same failure mode we re-test and update the verdict.
Affiliate links go to the retailer with the best current price, not the highest commission.

newsletter.title: The GymLedger weekly
newsletter.subtitle: Practical picks and straight answers from GymLedger, once a week. No spam.

defaultMetaDescription: Honest reviews of dumbbells, barbells, power racks, benches, kettlebells, and cardio machines. We tell you exactly what to buy at $500, $2,000, and $5,000 budgets — and what to skip regardless of budg

homepage.h1: Build your home gym right the first time.
homepage.subhead: Honest reviews of dumbbells, barbells, power racks, benches, kettlebells, and cardio machines. We tell you exactly what to buy at $500, $2,000, and $5,000 budgets — and what to skip regardless of budget.
homepage.trustLine: Every piece of equipment purchased at retail. No brand sponsorships. Affiliate revenue only on gear we'd buy again.

about.headline: Why GymLedger exists
about.body: The home gym market is full of $400 dumbbells that wobble and $2,000 racks that ship without hardware. We test equipment in a real garage gym, not a studio, and write about what actually holds up when you're using it 5 days a week.
